festivals.name: Midnight Sun Festival
festivals.description: Akureyri celebrates the summer solstice with concerts, art events, and outdoor festivities under 24-hour daylight.

destinations.name: Reykjavik
destinations.description: The world's northernmost capital combines colourful houses, vibrant art scene, and geothermal swimming pools.

destinations.name: Golden Circle
destinations.description: Iceland's most famous route connects the Þingvellir rift, Geysir hot spring, and Gullfoss waterfall.

destinations.name: South Coast
destinations.description: Dramatic black-sand beaches, thundering waterfalls Seljalandsfoss and Skógafoss, and glacier tongues.

destinations.name: Jökulsárlón Glacier Lagoon
destinations.description: A stunning glacial lagoon where house-sized icebergs calve from Breiðamerkurjökull and float to the sea.

destinations.name: Snæfellsnes Peninsula
destinations.description: A microcosm of Iceland's best scenery crowned by Snæfellsjökull glacier, Jules Verne's gateway to the earth's centre.

destinations.name: Akureyri & Mývatn
destinations.description: Iceland's northern capital near Lake Mývatn, ringed by pseudocraters, lava pillars, and geothermal bath.

destinations.name: Westfjords
destinations.description: Iceland's most dramatic and least-visited region, with towering bird cliffs at Látrabjarg and sweeping fjords.

destinations.name: Highlands (Landmannalaugar)
destinations.description: Iceland's interior wilderness of rhyolite mountains and natural hot springs, only accessible in summer.
